title: rubus serpens var. incultus
intro: Rubus serpens var. incultus, also known as the creeping raspberry, is a variant of the wild raspberry plant that has been naturalized in various parts of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral compounds. The creeping raspberry is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, offering several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain, and promote wound healing.
